name: Berries - Whole
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash 1 or 2 quarts of berries at a time.
2. Drain, cap, and stem if necessary.
3. For gooseberries, snip off heads and tails with scissors.
4. Prepare and boil preferred syrup, if desired.
5. Add 1/2 cup syrup, juice, or water to each clean jar.
Hot pack (for blueberries, currants, elderberries, gooseberries, and huckleberries):
6. Heat berries in boiling water for 30 seconds and drain.
7. Fill jars and cover with hot juice, leaving 1/2-inch headspace.
Raw pack (for any raw berries):
8. Fill jars with the raw berries, shaking gently while filling.
9. Cover with hot syrup, juice, or water, leaving 1/2-inch headspace.
10. Adjust lids and process according to the appropriate table for your canning method and altitude.
Processing directions:
- Table 1: Recommended process time for whole berries in a boiling-water canner (see below).
- Table 2: Process times for whole berries in a dial-gauge pressure canner (see below).
- Table 3: Process times for whole berries in a weighted-gauge pressure canner (see below).
Table 1. Recommended process time for Berries, Whole, in a boiling-water canner.
Style of Pack: Hot. Jar Size: Pints or Quarts.
Process Time at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 15 minutes
1,001 - 3,000 ft: 20 minutes
3,001 - 6,000 ft: 20 minutes
Above 6,000 ft: 25 minutes
Style of Pack: Raw. Jar Size: Pints.
Process Time at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 15 minutes
1,001 - 3,000 ft: 20 minutes
3,001 - 6,000 ft: 20 minutes
Above 6,000 ft: 25 minutes
Style of Pack: Raw. Jar Size: Quarts.
Process Time at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 20 minutes
1,001 - 3,000 ft: 25 minutes
3,001 - 6,000 ft: 30 minutes
Above 6,000 ft: 35 minutes
Table 2. Process Times for Berries, Whole, in a Dial-Gauge Pressure Canner.
Style of Pack: Hot. Jar Size: Pints or Quarts.
Process Time: 8 minutes
Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 2,000 ft: 6 lb
2,001 - 4,000 ft: 7 lb
4,001 - 6,000 ft: 8 lb
6,001 - 8,000 ft: 9 lb
Style of Pack: Raw. Jar Size: Pints, Quarts.
Process Time: 8 minutes (for pints), 10 minutes (for quarts)
Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 2,000 ft: 6 lb
2,001 - 4,000 ft: 7 lb
4,001 - 6,000 ft: 8 lb
6,001 - 8,000 ft: 9 lb
Table 3. Process Times for Berries, Whole, in a Weighted-Gauge Pressure Canner.
Style of Pack: Hot. Jar Size: Pints.
Process Time: 8 minutes
Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 5 lb
Above 1,000 ft: 10 lb
Style of Pack: Raw. Jar Size: Pints, Quarts.
Process Time: 8 minutes for pints, 10 minutes for quarts
Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 5 lb
Above 1,000 ft: 10 lb
